title: Leg ulcer skin review

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting underlying-condition context and clinician-assessed redness or swelling beyond the ulcer, warmth, increased pain, fever, severity, complication risk, and previous antibiotic exposure.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact leg-ulcer section supports documenting interval course, symptoms, functional effect, site, surrounding skin, warmth, spreading redness or swelling, pain, fever, underlying-condition and prior-antibiotic context without diagnosing infection.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting underlying-condition context and clinician-assessed redness or swelling beyond the ulcer, warmth, increased pain, fever, severity, complication risk, and previous antibiotic exposure.

source.title: Leg ulcer infection: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.3 — causes, colonisation, and infection signs

investigations.text: Supports documenting rapid worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, previous antibiotics, microbiology sampling already performed, and results already reviewed.
investigations.rationale: The reassessment section supports recording worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, disproportionate pain, previous antibiotic exposure and microbiology results already reviewed; no test or treatment is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting rapid worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, previous antibiotics, microbiology sampling already performed, and results already reviewed.

source.title: Leg ulcer infection: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations

referral.text: Supports recording clinician-identified serious illness, diabetes, immunosuppression, lymphangitis, spreading infection, or completed referral or specialist discussion; it does not create the referral.
referral.rationale: The referral section supports documenting clinician-discussed safety net and an arranged review or completed specialist discussion; it does not create referral or urgency.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician-identified serious illness, diabetes, immunosuppression, lymphangitis, spreading infection, or completed referral or specialist discussion; it does not create the referral.

source_limitations: Ulcer cause, exact site, dimensions, depth, wound bed, exudate, odour, pulses, perfusion, oedema, neuropathy, diabetes status, dressings, healing trajectory, diagnosis, vascular tests, treatment, compression, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
